sentence: Las casas están cerradas por el aguacero fuerte.
ipa: lah ˈkasah eˈstaŋ seˈraðah poɾ el aɣwaˈseɾo ˈfweɾte
gloss_en: The houses are shut because of the heavy downpour.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per hualde2005.

sentence: Mi pana comió arroz con habichuelas en el concho.
ipa: mi ˈpana koˈmio aˈros koŋ aβiˈtʃwelah eŋ el ˈkontʃo
gloss_en: My buddy ate rice with beans in the shared taxi.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per fontanella_de_weinberg1992.

sentence: El niño pequeño juega en el parque los domingos.
ipa: el ˈniɲo peˈkeɲo ˈhweɣa eŋ el ˈpaɾke loh doˈminɡoh
gloss_en: The little boy plays in the park on Sundays.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per penny2002.

sentence: ¿Vamos a coger la guagua para el centro?
ipa: ˈbamoh a koˈheɾ la ˈɡwaɣwa paɾa el ˈsentɾo
gloss_en: Shall we take the bus downtown?
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); seseo: ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s] per hualde2005.

sentence: Ese muchacho baila muy bien la bachata.
ipa: ˈese muˈtʃatʃo ˈbajla ˈmui ˈbjeŋ la baˈtʃata
gloss_en: That boy dances bachata really well.
notes: word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ]; Romance rising diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je/we] per fontanella_de_weinberg1992.

sentence: La luz del faro brilla sobre el malecón de noche.
ipa: la ˈlus del ˈfaɾo ˈbɾiʝa ˈsobɾe el maleˈkoŋ de ˈnotʃe
gloss_en: The lighthouse beam shines over the seafront at night.
notes: word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ]; seseo: ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s] per penny2002.

sentence: Compré diez guineos maduros en el colmado.
ipa: koˈmpɾe ˈdjes ˈɡineoh maˈðuɾoh eŋ el koˈlmaðo
gloss_en: I bought ten ripe bananas at the corner store.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per hualde2005.

sentence: El corazón de la ciudad late con merengue y baile.
ipa: el koɾaˈsoŋ de la sjuˈða ˈlate koŋ meˈɾenɡe i ˈbajle
gloss_en: The heart of the city beats with merengue and dance.
notes: word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ]; seseo: ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s] per fontanella_de_weinberg1992.

sentence: Los pescadores salen al amanecer con sus redes.
ipa: loh peskaˈðoɾeh ˈsaleŋ al amaneˈseɾ koŋ suh ˈreðeh
gloss_en: The fishermen set out at dawn with their nets.

sentence: ¿Cuántas personas vienen a la fiesta esta noche?
ipa: ˈkuantah peˈɾsonah ˈbjeneŋ a la ˈfjesta ˈesta ˈnotʃe
gloss_en: How many people are coming to the party tonight?
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per hualde2005.

sentence: La región del Cibao guarda muchos pueblos antiguos.
ipa: la reˈhioŋ del ˈsiβao ˈɡwaɾda ˈmutʃoh ˈpwebloh aˈntiɣwoh
gloss_en: The Cibao region holds many old villages.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per fontanella_de_weinberg1992.

sentence: El perro corre detrás del carro por la avenida.
ipa: el ˈpero ˈkore deˈtɾah del ˈkaro poɾ la aβeˈniða
gloss_en: The dog runs after the car along the avenue.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); alveolar trill [r] preserved per penny2002.

sentence: No quiero que llueva sobre las montañas hoy.
ipa: ˈno ˈkjeɾo ke ˈʝweβa ˈsobɾe lah moˈntaɲah ˈoi
gloss_en: I don't want it to rain over the mountains today.

sentence: Mi hermano vive lejos, cerca del río grande.
ipa: mi eˈɾmano ˈbiβe ˈlehoh ˈseɾka del ˈrio ˈɡɾande
gloss_en: My brother lives far away, near the big river.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); seseo: ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s] per fontanella_de_weinberg1992.

sentence: La mujer canta una canción vieja y hermosa.
ipa: la muˈheɾ ˈkanta una kaˈnsioŋ ˈbjeha i eˈɾmosa
gloss_en: The woman sings an old and beautiful song.

sentence: Dame un poco de agua bien fría, por favor.
ipa: ˈdame uŋ ˈpoko de ˈaɣwa ˈbjeŋ ˈfɾia poɾ faˈβoɾ
gloss_en: Give me a little very cold water, please.
notes: word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ]; Romance rising diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je/we] per hualde2005.

sentence: Aquellos árboles dan sombra en el conuco.
ipa: aˈkeʝoh ˈaɾboleh ˈdaŋ ˈsombɾa eŋ el koˈnuko
gloss_en: Those trees give shade over the small farm plot.

sentence: El señor mayor cuenta historias de su juventud.
ipa: el seˈɲoɾ maˈʝoɾ ˈkwenta iˈstoɾjah de su huβeˈntu
gloss_en: The old gentleman tells stories of his youth.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); yeísmo: ⟨ll/y⟩ realised [ʝ] per penny2002.

sentence: Las estrellas alumbran el campo durante la madrugada.
ipa: lah eˈstɾeʝah aˈlumbɾaŋ el ˈkampo duˈɾante la madɾuˈɣaða
gloss_en: The stars light up the countryside in the early morning.

sentence: Buenos días, ¿cómo amaneció usted por acá?
ipa: ˈbwenoh ˈdiah ˈkomo amaneˈsio uˈste poɾ aˈka
gloss_en: Good morning, how did you wake up around here?
